stories.title: Texas Tech Lands Moriah Polar — Nation's Top Hitter Completes Historic Transfer Class
stories.angle: Moriah Polar, the nation's leading hitter with a .555 batting average and 101 hits in 2026, committed to Texas Tech on Sunday night (June 15). Texas Tech confirmed the signing Monday morning. She joins Jayden Heavener (No. 1 pitcher in the portal, committed June 13) and Kasidi Pickering (No. 2 overall, DNT tag strongly pointing to Lubbock). If Pickering officially confirms, Jerry Glasco's Red Raiders — fresh off a WCWS runner-up finish — will have assembled arguably the most dominant transfer class in college softball history.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech just landed the nation's top hitter.

Moriah Polar: .555 BA, 101 hits, 67 runs in 2026. NFCA All-American.

Heavener (No. 1 pitcher) is already there. Pickering (No. 2) appears next.

Jerry Glasco is building something historic. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech's 2026 transfer haul, if it holds:

✅ Jayden Heavener — No. 1 pitcher in the portal
✅ Moriah Polar — nation's No. 1 hitter (.555 BA)
🔜 Kasidi Pickering — No. 2 overall (DNT tag)

The WCWS runner-up is not done. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Texas Tech Lands Moriah Polar — Nation's Top Hitter Joins Historic Transfer Class

stories.title: Torres Watch — Day 9, Six Days Left and No Decision
stories.angle: D1 Softball Player of the Year Isa Torres (.530 BA / .978 SLG / .591 OBP / 78 runs) enters Day 9 of the 15-day transfer portal window with no commitment. Texas and Texas A&M remain the frontrunners. The portal window closes June 22 — just six days away. The final week of the portal begins today, and the most coveted player in the transfer market has yet to reveal her destination.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Day 9. No commitment from Isa Torres.

.530 BA. .978 SLG. .591 OBP. 78 runs. D1 Player of the Year.

The final week of the portal starts today.

6 days left. June 22. Texas or Texas A&M — who wins? #SoftballTwitter

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Isa Torres still has the cards.

She owns every major FSU hitting record.
She set an NCAA record for hits in consecutive at-bats.
She is from Georgetown, TX — 30 min from UT.

6 days left. The clock is ticking. #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Torres Watch — Day 9: The Final Week Begins and the D1 Player of the Year Is Still Uncommitted

stories.title: Beachum — Day 9, No. 1 Transfer Stays Patient in Final Week
stories.angle: Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP) — ranked No. 1 overall transfer by Softball America — enters the final week of the portal window still without a commitment. Career: 37 HR, 180 RBI, 202 hits across three seasons at FSU. NFCA 2024 Freshman of the Year, 2026 Second-Team All-American. With Polar now off the board at Texas Tech, Beachum's decision carries even more weight for the programs still in the hunt.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Jaysoni Beachum is still out there.

Softball America's No. 1 transfer. .417 BA. 16 HR. 66 RBI. .549 OBP.
Career: 37 HR, 180 RBI, 202 hits. NFCA 2024 Freshman of the Year.

Whoever lands her wins the portal. 6 days left. #SoftballTwitter

stories.title: Georgia Enters the Mix — Kylie Hammonds Latest Power-Program Pickup
stories.angle: Georgia adds FAU transfer outfielder Kylie Hammonds as Power programs continue filling roster spots in the portal's second week. With Polar, Heavener, Hartley, Wetteland, Azua, Swan, Plumlee, and Altmeyer now committed, the depth tier of the portal is being claimed quickly. Programs that didn't land headliners are pivoting hard to find value before the June 22 deadline.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
While the headliners stay quiet, programs are still winning the portal.

Georgia adds Kylie Hammonds (FAU transfer, OF).

Depth moves matter. The portal closes June 22 — 6 days left. #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Georgia Adds Kylie Hammonds — How Programs Are Winning the Portal Without the Headline Names

stories.title: Portal Countdown — Day 9 of 15, Final 6 Days Begin
stories.angle: Day 9 of the 15-day transfer portal window. The final six days begin today. Portal closes June 22. Committed: Swan (Texas), Plumlee (Alabama), Altmeyer (Texas), Azua (Texas A&M), Hartley (OSU), Wetteland (Arkansas), Heavener (Texas Tech), Polar (Texas Tech). Still available: Torres (No. 1 / D1 POY), Beachum (No. 3 / Softball America No. 1). Appears decided (DNT): Pickering (No. 2, strongly linked to Texas Tech). The clock is ticking — who wins the final week?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Portal tracker — Day 9 of 15. 6 days left (closes June 22).

Committed: Swan, Plumlee, Altmeyer, Azua, Hartley, Wetteland, Heavener, Polar.

Still available: Torres (No. 1), Beachum (No. 3).
Appears decided: Pickering (No. 2).

Final week starts now. #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Portal Countdown — Day 9 of 15: Final Six Days Begin With Two Stars Still Uncommitted
